uplink.description: fPort 1 measurement: 3.5V battery, 868.43ppm CO2, 18.75°C, 44.29% RH (TTN example input)
uplink._note: Input is the TTN upstream example. expected.data authored by normalizing the upstream oracle output (battery V, co2 ppm, temperature °C, relativeHumidity %).

uplink.description: fPort 1 measurement with negative temperature: 3.0V battery, 10ppm CO2, -5.00°C, 25.00% RH
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; tempX100=0xFE0C>32767 exercises the two's-complement negative branch. expected.data normalized from upstream oracle output.

uplink.description: fPort 1 measurement with 'no temp' sentinel (0x7FFF): 2.8V battery, 5ppm CO2, 50.00% RH
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; tempX100=0x7FFF is the upstream 'no temp' sentinel. Upstream emits the string 'no temp'; vocabulary air.temperature is a number, so we omit it and flag the camelCase extra temperatureValid:false instead.

uplink.description: fPort 223 status frame: traffic-light state 10, 3.0V battery
uplink._note: Synthetic input; bytes[0]=0x8A: high bits 0xC0&==0x80 mark a status frame, low 6 bits = traffic-light state 10, low nibble 0x0A => battery 3.0V. Upstream type:'status'/status:N normalized to camelCase extras messageType/trafficLightState.
Synthetic input; on an unrecognized fPort upstream decodes only the battery nibble and returns the rest empty. We mirror that, emitting battery only.

uplink.description: error: fPort 1 frame too short to hold a full measurement
uplink._note: Synthetic malformed input; a fPort 1 measurement needs 10 bytes. Upstream would read undefined bytes; our codec rejects the truncated frame.
